The calendar, in that case, could apply to multiple time periods, like working hours, automatic door times and automatic area arm times, each reacting to the same calendar. A user can then update the calendar throughout the year and the system reacts correctly. Another example is public holidays, a calendar can include holidays that always fall on the same day each year, such as Christmas and New Year’s Eve and have them repeat, as well as being manually updated each year with holidays that change like Easter. The calendar exception could prevent the time period from being valid on a particular day and as such the normal operations would not apply to such things as user permissions or door free access times.

313514FD278BEFAFF64C09264D5E8B35: - Configures the input as a breakglass and once set you will need to link this input to a door.  Emergency breakglass is often wired directly into the door's power circuit, allowing the door to be unlocked in case of emergency. This however bypasses the lock output state so even if the controller says the door is locked, the door can actually be opened. By wiring the breakglass detector into the system and linking it to a door, the door will be marked as unlocked and identify the cause as the breakglass. A warning is also created, prompting that the breakglass should be fixed and that the door is not secure.
